396	                "`self.config_name` is not defined. Note that one should not load a config from "

414	            elif os.path.isfile(os.path.join(pretrained_model_name_or_path, cls.config_name)):

498	    @classmethod
499	    def extract_init_dict(cls, config_dict, **kwargs):
500	        # Skip keys that were not present in the original config, so default __init__ values were used
501	        used_defaults = config_dict.get("_use_default_values", [])
502	        config_dict = {k: v for k, v in config_dict.items() if k not in used_defaults and k != "_use_default_values"}
503	
504	        # 0. Copy origin config dict
505	        original_dict = dict(config_dict.items())
506	
507	        # 1. Retrieve expected config attributes from __init__ signature
508	        expected_keys = cls._get_init_keys(cls)
509	        expected_keys.remove("self")
510	        # remove general kwargs if present in dict
511	        if "kwargs" in expected_keys:
512	            expected_keys.remove("kwargs")
513	        # remove flax internal keys
514	        if hasattr(cls, "_flax_internal_args"):
515	            for arg in cls._flax_internal_args:
516	                expected_keys.remove(arg)
517	
518	        # 2. Remove attributes that cannot be expected from expected config attributes
519	        # remove keys to be ignored
520	        if len(cls.ignore_for_config) > 0:
521	            expected_keys = expected_keys - set(cls.ignore_for_config)
522	
523	        # load diffusers library to import compatible and original scheduler
524	        diffusers_library = importlib.import_module(__name__.split(".")[0])
525	
526	        if cls.has_compatibles:
527	            compatible_classes = [c for c in cls._get_compatibles() if not isinstance(c, DummyObject)]
528	        else:
529	            compatible_classes = []
530	
531	        expected_keys_comp_cls = set()
532	        for c in compatible_classes:
533	            expected_keys_c = cls._get_init_keys(c)
534	            expected_keys_comp_cls = expected_keys_comp_cls.union(expected_keys_c)
535	        expected_keys_comp_cls = expected_keys_comp_cls - cls._get_init_keys(cls)
536	        config_dict = {k: v for k, v in config_dict.items() if k not in expected_keys_comp_cls}
537	
538	        # remove attributes from orig class that cannot be expected
539	        orig_cls_name = config_dict.pop("_class_name", cls.__name__)
540	        if (
541	            isinstance(orig_cls_name, str)
542	            and orig_cls_name != cls.__name__
543	            and hasattr(diffusers_library, orig_cls_name)
544	        ):
545	            orig_cls = getattr(diffusers_library, orig_cls_name)
546	            unexpected_keys_from_orig = cls._get_init_keys(orig_cls) - expected_keys
547	            config_dict = {k: v for k, v in config_dict.items() if k not in unexpected_keys_from_orig}
548	        elif not isinstance(orig_cls_name, str) and not isinstance(orig_cls_name, (list, tuple)):
549	            raise ValueError(
550	                "Make sure that the `_class_name` is of type string or list of string (for custom pipelines)."
551	            )
552	
553	        # remove private attributes
554	        config_dict = {k: v for k, v in config_dict.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
555	
556	        # remove quantization_config
557	        config_dict = {k: v for k, v in config_dict.items() if k != "quantization_config"}
558	
559	        # 3. Create keyword arguments that will be passed to __init__ from expected keyword arguments
560	        init_dict = {}
561	        for key in expected_keys:
562	            # if config param is passed to kwarg and is present in config dict
563	            # it should overwrite existing config dict key
564	            if key in kwargs and key in config_dict:
565	                config_dict[key] = kwargs.pop(key)
566	
567	            if key in kwargs:
568	                # overwrite key
569	                init_dict[key] = kwargs.pop(key)
570	            elif key in config_dict:
571	                # use value from config dict
572	                init_dict[key] = config_dict.pop(key)
573	
574	        # 4. Give nice warning if unexpected values have been passed
575	        if len(config_dict) > 0:
576	            logger.warning(
577	                f"The config attributes {config_dict} were passed to {cls.__name__}, "
578	                "but are not expected and will be ignored. Please verify your "
579	                f"{cls.config_name} configuration file."
580	            )
581	
582	        # 5. Give nice info if config attributes are initialized to default because they have not been passed
583	        passed_keys = set(init_dict.keys())
584	        if len(expected_keys - passed_keys) > 0:
585	            logger.info(
586	                f"{expected_keys - passed_keys} was not found in config. Values will be initialized to default values."
587	            )
588	
589	        # 6. Define unused keyword arguments
590	        unused_kwargs = {**config_dict, **kwargs}
591	
592	        # 7. Define "hidden" config parameters that were saved for compatible classes
593	        hidden_config_dict = {k: v for k, v in original_dict.items() if k not in init_dict}
594	
595	        return init_dict, unused_kwargs, hidden_config_dict

619	    def to_json_string(self) -> str:
620	        """
621	        Serializes the configuration instance to a JSON string.
622	
623	        Returns:
624	            `str`:
625	                String containing all the attributes that make up the configuration instance in JSON format.
626	        """
627	        config_dict = self._internal_dict if hasattr(self, "_internal_dict") else {}
628	        config_dict["_class_name"] = self.__class__.__name__
629	        config_dict["_diffusers_version"] = __version__
630	
631	        def to_json_saveable(value):
632	            if isinstance(value, np.ndarray):
633	                value = value.tolist()
634	            elif isinstance(value, Path):
635	                value = value.as_posix()
636	            elif hasattr(value, "to_dict") and callable(value.to_dict):
637	                value = value.to_dict()
638	            elif isinstance(value, list):
639	                value = [to_json_saveable(v) for v in value]
640	            return value
641	
642	        if "quantization_config" in config_dict:
643	            config_dict["quantization_config"] = (
644	                config_dict.quantization_config.to_dict()
645	                if not isinstance(config_dict.quantization_config, dict)
646	                else config_dict.quantization_config
647	            )
648	
649	        config_dict = {k: to_json_saveable(v) for k, v in config_dict.items()}
650	        # Don't save "_ignore_files" or "_use_default_values"
651	        config_dict.pop("_ignore_files", None)
652	        config_dict.pop("_use_default_values", None)
653	        # pop the `_pre_quantization_dtype` as torch.dtypes are not serializable.
654	        _ = config_dict.pop("_pre_quantization_dtype", None)
655	
656	        if getattr(self, "_auto_class", None) is not None:
657	            module = self.__class__.__module__.split(".")[-1]
658	            auto_map = config_dict.get("auto_map", {})
659	            auto_map[self._auto_class] = f"{module}.{self.__class__.__name__}"
660	            config_dict["auto_map"] = auto_map
661	
662	        return json.dumps(config_dict, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n"

670	                Path to the JSON file to save a configuration instance's parameters.
671	        """
672	        with open(json_file_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as writer:

690	def register_to_config(init):
691	    r"""
692	    Decorator to apply on the init of classes inheriting from [`ConfigMixin`] so that all the arguments are
693	    automatically sent to `self.register_for_config`. To ignore a specific argument accepted by the init but that
694	    shouldn't be registered in the config, use the `ignore_for_config` class variable
695	
696	    Warning: Once decorated, all private arguments (beginning with an underscore) are trashed and not sent to the init!
697	    """
698	
699	    @functools.wraps(init)

699	    @functools.wraps(init)
700	    def inner_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
701	        # Ignore private kwargs in the init.
702	        init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
703	        config_init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k.startswith("_")}
704	        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
705	            raise RuntimeError(
706	                f"`@register_for_config` was applied to {self.__class__.__name__} init method, but this class does "
707	                "not inherit from `ConfigMixin`."
708	            )
709	
710	        ignore = getattr(self, "ignore_for_config", [])
711	        # Get positional arguments aligned with kwargs
712	        new_kwargs = {}
713	        signature = inspect.signature(init)
714	        parameters = {
715	            name: p.default for i, (name, p) in enumerate(signature.parameters.items()) if i > 0 and name not in ignore
716	        }
717	        for arg, name in zip(args, parameters.keys()):
718	            new_kwargs[name] = arg
719	
720	        # Then add all kwargs
721	        new_kwargs.update(
722	            {
723	                k: init_kwargs.get(k, default)
724	                for k, default in parameters.items()
725	                if k not in ignore and k not in new_kwargs
726	            }
727	        )
728	
729	        # Take note of the parameters that were not present in the loaded config
730	        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
731	            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))
732	
733	        new_kwargs = {**config_init_kwargs, **new_kwargs}
734	        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
735	        init(self, *args, **init_kwargs)
736	
737	    return inner_init
738

740	def flax_register_to_config(cls):
741	    original_init = cls.__init__
742	
743	    @functools.wraps(original_init)
744	    def init(self, *args, **kwargs):
745	        if not isinstance(self, ConfigMixin):
746	            raise RuntimeError(
747	                f"`@register_for_config` was applied to {self.__class__.__name__} init method, but this class does "
748	                "not inherit from `ConfigMixin`."
749	            )
750	
751	        # Ignore private kwargs in the init. Retrieve all passed attributes
752	        init_kwargs = dict(kwargs.items())
753	
754	        # Retrieve default values
755	        fields = dataclasses.fields(self)
756	        default_kwargs = {}
757	        for field in fields:
758	            # ignore flax specific attributes
759	            if field.name in self._flax_internal_args:
760	                continue
761	            if type(field.default) == dataclasses._MISSING_TYPE:
762	                default_kwargs[field.name] = None
763	            else:
764	                default_kwargs[field.name] = getattr(self, field.name)
765	
766	        # Make sure init_kwargs override default kwargs
767	        new_kwargs = {**default_kwargs, **init_kwargs}
768	        # dtype should be part of `init_kwargs`, but not `new_kwargs`
769	        if "dtype" in new_kwargs:
770	            new_kwargs.pop("dtype")
771	
772	        # Get positional arguments aligned with kwargs
773	        for i, arg in enumerate(args):
774	            name = fields[i].name
775	            new_kwargs[name] = arg
776	
777	        # Take note of the parameters that were not present in the loaded config
778	        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
779	            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))
780	
781	        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
782	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
783	
784	    cls.__init__ = init
785	    return cls

34	def betas_for_alpha_bar(
35	    num_diffusion_timesteps: int,
36	    max_beta: float = 0.999,
37	    alpha_transform_type: Literal["cosine", "exp", "laplace"] = "cosine",
38	) -> torch.Tensor:
39	    """
40	    Create a beta schedule that discretizes the given alpha_t_bar function, which defines the cumulative product of
41	    (1-beta) over time from t = [0,1].
42	
43	    Contains a function alpha_bar that takes an argument t and transforms it to the cumulative product of (1-beta) up
44	    to that part of the diffusion process.
45	
46	    Args:
47	        num_diffusion_timesteps (`int`):
48	            The number of betas to produce.
49	        max_beta (`float`, defaults to `0.999`):
50	            The maximum beta to use; use values lower than 1 to avoid numerical instability.
51	        alpha_transform_type (`str`, defaults to `"cosine"`):
52	            The type of noise schedule for `alpha_bar`. Choose from `cosine`, `exp`, or `laplace`.
53	
54	    Returns:
55	        `torch.Tensor`:
56	            The betas used by the scheduler to step the model outputs.
57	    """
58	    if alpha_transform_type == "cosine":
59	
60	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
61	            return math.cos((t + 0.008) / 1.008 * math.pi / 2) ** 2
62	
63	    elif alpha_transform_type == "laplace":
64	
65	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
66	            lmb = -0.5 * math.copysign(1, 0.5 - t) * math.log(1 - 2 * math.fabs(0.5 - t) + 1e-6)
67	            snr = math.exp(lmb)
68	            return math.sqrt(snr / (1 + snr))
69	
70	    elif alpha_transform_type == "exp":
71	
72	        def alpha_bar_fn(t):
73	            return math.exp(t * -12.0)
74	
75	    else:
76	        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported alpha_transform_type: {alpha_transform_type}")
77	
78	    betas = []
79	    for i in range(num_diffusion_timesteps):
80	        t1 = i / num_diffusion_timesteps
81	        t2 = (i + 1) / num_diffusion_timesteps
82	        betas.append(min(1 - alpha_bar_fn(t2) / alpha_bar_fn(t1), max_beta))
83	    return torch.tensor(betas, dtype=torch.float32)
84	
85	
86	# Copied from diffusers.schedulers.scheduling_ddim.rescale_zero_terminal_snr
87	def rescale_zero_terminal_snr(betas):
88	    """
89	    Rescales betas to have zero terminal SNR Based on https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891 (Algorithm 1)
90	
91	    Args:
92	        betas (`torch.Tensor`):
93	            The betas that the scheduler is being initialized with.
94	
95	    Returns:
96	        `torch.Tensor`:
97	            Rescaled betas with zero terminal SNR.
98	    """
99	    # Convert betas to alphas_bar_sqrt
100	    alphas = 1.0 - betas
101	    alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(alphas, dim=0)
102	    alphas_bar_sqrt = alphas_cumprod.sqrt()
103	
104	    # Store old values.
105	    alphas_bar_sqrt_0 = alphas_bar_sqrt[0].clone()
106	    alphas_bar_sqrt_T = alphas_bar_sqrt[-1].clone()
107	
108	    # Shift so the last timestep is zero.
109	    alphas_bar_sqrt -= alphas_bar_sqrt_T
110	
111	    # Scale so the first timestep is back to the old value.
112	    alphas_bar_sqrt *= alphas_bar_sqrt_0 / (alphas_bar_sqrt_0 - alphas_bar_sqrt_T)
113	
114	    # Convert alphas_bar_sqrt to betas
115	    alphas_bar = alphas_bar_sqrt**2  # Revert sqrt
116	    alphas = alphas_bar[1:] / alphas_bar[:-1]  # Revert cumprod
117	    alphas = torch.cat([alphas_bar[0:1], alphas])
118	    betas = 1 - alphas
119	
120	    return betas
121	
122	
123	class DPMSolverMultistepScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
124	    """
125	    `DPMSolverMultistepScheduler` is a fast dedicated high-order solver for diffusion ODEs.
126	
127	    This model inherits from [`SchedulerMixin`] and [`ConfigMixin`]. Check the superclass documentation for the generic
128	    methods the library implements for all schedulers such as loading and saving.
129	
130	    Args:
131	        num_train_timesteps (`int`, defaults to 1000):
132	            The number of diffusion steps to train the model.
133	        beta_start (`float`, defaults to 0.0001):
134	            The starting `beta` value of inference.
135	        beta_end (`float`, defaults to 0.02):
136	            The final `beta` value.
137	        beta_schedule (`"linear"`, `"scaled_linear"`, or `"squaredcos_cap_v2"`, defaults to `"linear"`):
138	            The beta schedule, a mapping from a beta range to a sequence of betas for stepping the model.
139	        trained_betas (`np.ndarray`, *optional*):
140	            Pass an array of betas directly to the constructor to bypass `beta_start` and `beta_end`.
141	        solver_order (`int`, defaults to 2):
142	            The DPMSolver order which can be `1` or `2` or `3`. It is recommended to use `solver_order=2` for guided
143	            sampling, and `solver_order=3` for unconditional sampling.
144	        prediction_type (`"epsilon"`, `"sample"`, `"v_prediction"`, or `"flow_prediction"`, defaults to `"epsilon"`):
145	            Prediction type of the scheduler function. `epsilon` predicts the noise of the diffusion process, `sample`
146	            directly predicts the noisy sample, `v_prediction` predicts the velocity (see section 2.4 of [Imagen
147	            Video](https://huggingface.co/papers/2210.02303) paper), and `flow_prediction` predicts the flow.
148	        thresholding (`bool`, defaults to `False`):
149	            Whether to use the "dynamic thresholding" method. This is unsuitable for latent-space diffusion models such
150	            as Stable Diffusion.
151	        dynamic_thresholding_ratio (`float`, defaults to 0.995):
152	            The ratio for the dynamic thresholding method. Valid only when `thresholding=True`.
153	        sample_max_value (`float`, defaults to 1.0):
154	            The threshold value for dynamic thresholding. Valid only when `thresholding=True` and
155	            `algorithm_type="dpmsolver++"`.
156	        algorithm_type (`"dpmsolver"`, `"dpmsolver++"`, `"sde-dpmsolver"`, or `"sde-dpmsolver++"`, defaults to `"dpmsolver++"`):
157	            Algorithm type for the solver. The `dpmsolver` type implements the algorithms in the
158	            [DPMSolver](https://huggingface.co/papers/2206.00927) paper, and the `dpmsolver++` type implements the
159	            algorithms in the [DPMSolver++](https://huggingface.co/papers/2211.01095) paper. It is recommended to use
160	            `dpmsolver++` or `sde-dpmsolver++` with `solver_order=2` for guided sampling like in Stable Diffusion.
161	        solver_type (`"midpoint"` or `"heun"`, defaults to `"midpoint"`):
162	            Solver type for the second-order solver. The solver type slightly affects the sample quality, especially
163	            for a small number of steps. It is recommended to use `midpoint` solvers.
164	        lower_order_final (`bool`, defaults to `True`):
165	            Whether to use lower-order solvers in the final steps. Only valid for < 15 inference steps. This can
166	            stabilize the sampling of DPMSolver for steps < 15, especially for steps <= 10.
167	        euler_at_final (`bool`, defaults to `False`):
168	            Whether to use Euler's method in the final step. It is a trade-off between numerical stability and detail
169	            richness. This can stabilize the sampling of the SDE variant of DPMSolver for small number of inference
170	            steps, but sometimes may result in blurring.
171	        use_karras_sigmas (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
172	            Whether to use Karras sigmas for step sizes in the noise schedule during the sampling process. If `True`,
173	            the sigmas are determined according to a sequence of noise levels {σi}.
174	        use_exponential_sigmas (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
175	            Whether to use exponential sigmas for step sizes in the noise schedule during the sampling process.
176	        use_beta_sigmas (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
177	            Whether to use beta sigmas for step sizes in the noise schedule during the sampling process. Refer to [Beta
178	            Sampling is All You Need](https://huggingface.co/papers/2407.12173) for more information.
179	        use_lu_lambdas (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
180	            Whether to use the uniform-logSNR for step sizes proposed by Lu's DPM-Solver in the noise schedule during
181	            the sampling process. If `True`, the sigmas and time steps are determined according to a sequence of
182	            `lambda(t)`.
183	        use_flow_sigmas (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
184	            Whether to use flow sigmas for step sizes in the noise schedule during the sampling process.
185	        flow_shift (`float`, *optional*, defaults to 1.0):
186	            The shift value for the timestep schedule for flow matching.
187	        final_sigmas_type (`"zero"` or `"sigma_min"`, *optional*, defaults to `"zero"`):
188	            The final `sigma` value for the noise schedule during the sampling process. If `"sigma_min"`, the final
189	            sigma is the same as the last sigma in the training schedule. If `"zero"`, the final sigma is set to 0.
190	        lambda_min_clipped (`float`, defaults to `-inf`):
191	            Clipping threshold for the minimum value of `lambda(t)` for numerical stability. This is critical for the
192	            cosine (`squaredcos_cap_v2`) noise schedule.
193	        variance_type (`"learned"` or `"learned_range"`, *optional*):
194	            Set to `"learned"` or `"learned_range"` for diffusion models that predict variance. If set, the model's
195	            output contains the predicted Gaussian variance.
196	        timestep_spacing (`"linspace"`, `"leading"`, or `"trailing"`, defaults to `"linspace"`):
197	            The way the timesteps should be scaled. Refer to Table 2 of the [Common Diffusion Noise Schedules and
198	            Sample Steps are Flawed](https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891) for more information.
199	        steps_offset (`int`, defaults to 0):
200	            An offset added to the inference steps, as required by some model families.
201	        rescale_betas_zero_snr (`bool`, defaults to `False`):
202	            Whether to rescale the betas to have zero terminal SNR. This enables the model to generate very bright and
203	            dark samples instead of limiting it to samples with medium brightness. Loosely related to
204	            [`--offset_noise`](https://github.com/huggingface/diffusers/blob/74fd735eb073eb1d774b1ab4154a0876eb82f055/examples/dreambooth/train_dreambooth.py#L506).
205	        use_dynamic_shifting (`bool`, defaults to `False`):
206	            Whether to use dynamic shifting for the timestep schedule.
207	        time_shift_type (`"exponential"`, defaults to `"exponential"`):
208	            The type of time shift to apply when using dynamic shifting.
209	    """
210	
211	    _compatibles = [e.name for e in KarrasDiffusionSchedulers]
212	    order = 1
213	
214	    @register_to_config
215	    def __init__(
216	        self,
217	        num_train_timesteps: int = 1000,
218	        beta_start: float = 0.0001,
219	        beta_end: float = 0.02,
220	        beta_schedule: str = "linear",
221	        trained_betas: np.ndarray | list[float] | None = None,
222	        solver_order: int = 2,
223	        prediction_type: Literal["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction", "flow_prediction"] = "epsilon",
224	        thresholding: bool = False,
225	        dynamic_thresholding_ratio: float = 0.995,
226	        sample_max_value: float = 1.0,
227	        algorithm_type: Literal["dpmsolver", "dpmsolver++", "sde-dpmsolver", "sde-dpmsolver++"] = "dpmsolver++",
228	        solver_type: Literal["midpoint", "heun"] = "midpoint",
229	        lower_order_final: bool = True,
230	        euler_at_final: bool = False,
231	        use_karras_sigmas: bool = False,
232	        use_exponential_sigmas: bool = False,
233	        use_beta_sigmas: bool = False,
234	        use_lu_lambdas: bool = False,
235	        use_flow_sigmas: bool = False,
236	        flow_shift: float = 1.0,
237	        final_sigmas_type: Literal["zero", "sigma_min"] = "zero",
238	        lambda_min_clipped: float = -float("inf"),
239	        variance_type: Literal["learned", "learned_range"] | None = None,
240	        timestep_spacing: Literal["linspace", "leading", "trailing"] = "linspace",
241	        steps_offset: int = 0,
242	        rescale_betas_zero_snr: bool = False,
243	        use_dynamic_shifting: bool = False,
244	        time_shift_type: Literal["exponential"] = "exponential",
245	    ):
246	        if self.config.use_beta_sigmas and not is_scipy_available():
247	            raise ImportError("Make sure to install scipy if you want to use beta sigmas.")
248	        if (
249	            sum(
250	                [
251	                    self.config.use_beta_sigmas,
252	                    self.config.use_exponential_sigmas,
253	                    self.config.use_karras_sigmas,
254	                ]
255	            )
256	            > 1
257	        ):
258	            raise ValueError(
259	                "Only one of `config.use_beta_sigmas`, `config.use_exponential_sigmas`, `config.use_karras_sigmas` can be used."
260	            )
261	        if algorithm_type in ["dpmsolver", "sde-dpmsolver"]:
262	            deprecation_message = f"algorithm_type {algorithm_type} is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Choose from `dpmsolver++` or `sde-dpmsolver++` instead"
263	            deprecate(
264	                "algorithm_types dpmsolver and sde-dpmsolver",
265	                "1.0.0",
266	                deprecation_message,
267	            )
268	
269	        if trained_betas is not None:
270	            self.betas = torch.tensor(trained_betas, dtype=torch.float32)
271	        elif beta_schedule == "linear":
272	            self.betas = torch.linspace(beta_start, beta_end, num_train_timesteps, dtype=torch.float32)
273	        elif beta_schedule == "scaled_linear":
274	            # this schedule is very specific to the latent diffusion model.
275	            self.betas = (
276	                torch.linspace(
277	                    beta_start**0.5,
278	                    beta_end**0.5,
279	                    num_train_timesteps,
280	                    dtype=torch.float32,
281	                )
282	                ** 2
283	            )
284	        elif beta_schedule == "squaredcos_cap_v2":
285	            # Glide cosine schedule
286	            self.betas = betas_for_alpha_bar(num_train_timesteps)
287	        else:
288	            raise NotImplementedError(f"{beta_schedule} is not implemented for {self.__class__}")
289	
290	        if rescale_betas_zero_snr:
291	            self.betas = rescale_zero_terminal_snr(self.betas)
292	
293	        self.alphas = 1.0 - self.betas
294	        self.alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(self.alphas, dim=0)
295	
296	        if rescale_betas_zero_snr:
297	            # Close to 0 without being 0 so first sigma is not inf
298	            # FP16 smallest positive subnormal works well here
299	            self.alphas_cumprod[-1] = 2**-24
300

Perfect! Now let me compile a comprehensive report with all the findings.

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Scheduler Implementation in Hugging Face Diffusers

I've conducted a thorough exploration of the scheduler architecture in the Hugging Face diffusers library. Here are my detailed findings:

### 1. SCHEDULER FILES DIRECTORY

The `/src/diffusers/schedulers/` directory contains **57 scheduler files**:

**Core Base Classes:**
- `scheduling_utils.py` - Base classes and utilities
- `configuration_utils.py` (parent) - Configuration management

**Main Schedulers (PyTorch):**
- `scheduling_ddpm.py` - Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
- `scheduling_ddim.py` - DDIM variant
- `scheduling_dpmsolver_multistep.py` - DPM-Solver multi-step
- `scheduling_euler_discrete.py` - Euler method
- `scheduling_heun_discrete.py` - Heun method
- `scheduling_pndm.py` - PNDM scheduler
- And 40+ additional specialized schedulers

**Flax Variants (JAX):**
- `scheduling_ddpm_flax.py`
- `scheduling_ddim_flax.py`
- `scheduling_euler_discrete_flax.py`
- And others for different backends

### 2. ARCHITECTURE & INHERITANCE

**Class Hierarchy Pattern:**
```python
class [SchedulerName]Scheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
    ...
```

**Base Classes Location:**
- `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py` - SchedulerMixin
- `/src/diffusers/configuration_utils.py` - ConfigMixin

### 3. DETAILED SCHEDULER IMPLEMENTATION (DDPMScheduler Example)

**File:** `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddpm.py`

#### Class Structure:
```python
class DDPMScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
    _compatibles = [e.name for e in KarrasDiffusionSchedulers]
    order = 1
```

#### `@register_to_config` Decorator:
Applied to `__init__` method. This decorator automatically registers all init parameters to the config dictionary, which allows:
- Config saving/loading
- Parameter serialization to JSON
- Automatic state persistence

#### `__init__` Signature (Lines 186-211):
```python
@register_to_config
def __init__(
    self,
    num_train_timesteps: int = 1000,
    beta_start: float = 0.0001,
    beta_end: float = 0.02,
    beta_schedule: Literal["linear", "scaled_linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2", "sigmoid"] = "linear",
    trained_betas: np.ndarray | list[float] | None = None,
    variance_type: Literal[...] = "fixed_small",
    clip_sample: bool = True,
    prediction_type: Literal["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"] = "epsilon",
    thresholding: bool = False,
    dynamic_thresholding_ratio: float = 0.995,
    clip_sample_range: float = 1.0,
    sample_max_value: float = 1.0,
    timestep_spacing: Literal["linspace", "leading", "trailing"] = "leading",
    steps_offset: int = 0,
    rescale_betas_zero_snr: bool = False,
):
```

#### Beta Schedule Computation (Lines 212-241):
The scheduler supports multiple beta schedule types:
- **"linear"**: `torch.linspace(beta_start, beta_end, num_train_timesteps)`
- **"scaled_linear"**: Square root scaling for latent diffusion
- **"squaredcos_cap_v2"**: Glide cosine schedule via `betas_for_alpha_bar()`
- **"sigmoid"**: GeoDiff sigmoid schedule
- **"trained_betas"**: Custom pre-computed betas array

**Helper Function:** `betas_for_alpha_bar()` (Lines 48-97):
- Creates beta schedule from alpha_bar function
- Supports "cosine", "laplace", "exp" alpha transform types
- Returns torch.Tensor of shape (num_diffusion_timesteps,)

**Zero SNR Rescaling:** `rescale_zero_terminal_snr()` (Lines 101-134):
- Rescales betas for improved sample quality
- Enables very bright and dark samples

#### Alpha Computations (Lines 243-245):
```python
self.alphas = 1.0 - self.betas
self.alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(self.alphas, dim=0)  # Cumulative product
```

#### Key Instance Variables Set in `__init__`:
```python
self.init_noise_sigma = 1.0  # Standard deviation of initial noise
self.custom_timesteps = False
self.num_inference_steps = None
self.timesteps = torch.from_numpy(np.arange(0, num_train_timesteps)[::-1].copy())
self.variance_type = variance_type
```

#### `set_timesteps()` Method (Lines 274-346):

**Signature:**
```python
def set_timesteps(
    self,
    num_inference_steps: int = None,
    device: str | torch.device = None,
    timesteps: list[int] | None = None,
) -> None:
```

**Functionality:**
- Sets discrete timesteps for the denoising chain
- **Must be called before inference**
- Supports custom timesteps or automatic spacing
- Three spacing strategies matching Table 2 of https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891:
  - **"linspace"**: Evenly spaced across range
  - **"leading"**: Leading edge spacing (step_ratio = num_train_timesteps // num_inference_steps)
  - **"trailing"**: Trailing edge spacing

#### `step()` Method (Lines 461-567):

**Signature:**
```python
def step(
    self,
    model_output: torch.Tensor,
    timestep: int,
    sample: torch.Tensor,
    generator: torch.Generator | None = None,
    return_dict: bool = True,
) -> DDPMSchedulerOutput | tuple:
```

**Process (7 steps):**
1. Extract variance predictions from model output (if using learned variance)
2. Compute alpha products at current and previous timesteps
3. Convert model output to predicted original sample based on `prediction_type`:
   - "epsilon": `(sample - sqrt(beta_prod_t) * model_output) / sqrt(alpha_prod_t)`
   - "sample": Use directly
   - "v_prediction": `(sqrt(alpha_prod_t) * sample) - (sqrt(beta_prod_t) * model_output)`
4. Apply thresholding or clipping to predicted sample
5. Compute coefficients for prev_sample computation
6. Compute predicted previous sample µ_t
7. Add noise (Gaussian noise scaled by variance)

**Return Type:** `DDPMSchedulerOutput` dataclass (Lines 31-45):
```python
@dataclass
class DDPMSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
    prev_sample: torch.Tensor
    pred_original_sample: torch.Tensor | None = None
```

#### `add_noise()` Method (Lines 569-609):

**Signature:**
```python
def add_noise(
    self,
    original_samples: torch.Tensor,
    noise: torch.Tensor,
    timesteps: torch.IntTensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
```

**Process (Forward Diffusion):**
```python
sqrt_alpha_prod = alphas_cumprod[timesteps] ** 0.5
sqrt_one_minus_alpha_prod = (1 - alphas_cumprod[timesteps]) ** 0.5
noisy_samples = sqrt_alpha_prod * original_samples + sqrt_one_minus_alpha_prod * noise
```

**Pattern:** Reshape to broadcast correctly across batch dimensions.

#### Helper Methods:

**`_get_variance()` (Lines 348-416):**
- Computes variance for given timestep
- Supports 6 variance types: fixed_small, fixed_small_log, fixed_large, fixed_large_log, learned, learned_range

**`_threshold_sample()` (Lines 418-459):**
- Dynamic thresholding based on quantile values
- Prevents pixel saturation

**`previous_timestep()` (Lines 648-668):**
- Computes previous timestep accounting for custom timesteps

**`scale_model_input()` (Lines 257-272):**

**Key Differences from DDPM:**

### 6. CONFIGMIXIN CLASS

**File:** `/src/diffusers/configuration_utils.py`

#### ConfigMixin Class (Lines 88-189):

**Key Features:**

**Methods:**

```python
@property
```

#### `register_to_config` Decorator (Lines 690-737):

**Location:** `/src/diffusers/configuration_utils.py`, lines 690-737

**Implementation:**
```python
def register_to_config(init):
    @functools.wraps(init)
    def inner_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
        # 1. Separate private kwargs (start with _)
        init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if not k.startswith("_")}
        config_init_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k.startswith("_")}
        
        # 2. Get init signature and extract parameters
        signature = inspect.signature(init)
        parameters = {name: p.default for name, p in signature.parameters.items()}
        
        # 3. Map positional args to kwargs
        new_kwargs = {}
        for arg, name in zip(args, parameters.keys()):
            new_kwargs[name] = arg
        
        # 4. Track which params used defaults vs were passed
        if len(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs)) > 0:
            new_kwargs["_use_default_values"] = list(set(new_kwargs.keys()) - set(init_kwargs))
        
        # 5. Register all to config (except those in ignore_for_config)
        getattr(self, "register_to_config")(**new_kwargs)
        
        # 6. Call original __init__
        init(self, *args, **init_kwargs)
    
    return inner_init
```

**Behavior:**
- Automatically captures all init parameters
- Registers them to `self._internal_dict`
- Filters out ignored parameters
- Calls `self.register_to_config()` with captured kwargs
- Then calls original `__init__` with actual parameters

#### FrozenDict Class (Lines 56-85):
- Immutable OrderedDict
- Prevents modification after creation
- Raises exceptions on del, setdefault, pop, update, __setitem__

---

### 7. KEY PATTERNS & CONVENTIONS

#### Pattern 1: Output Dataclasses
All scheduler outputs are `@dataclass` inheriting from `BaseOutput`:
```python

class [SchedulerName]SchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
    prev_sample: torch.Tensor
    [optional_fields]: [Type] | None = None
```

#### Pattern 2: Beta Schedule Registration

```python
    self.betas = torch.tensor(trained_betas, dtype=torch.float32)
elif beta_schedule == "linear":
    self.betas = torch.linspace(beta_start, beta_end, num_train_timesteps, dtype=torch.float32)
# ... more schedule types
else:
    raise NotImplementedError(f"{beta_schedule} is not implemented for {self.__class__}")
```

#### Pattern 3: Config Access
```python

self.config.num_train_timesteps
self.config.prediction_type
self.config.clip_sample
```

#### Pattern 4: Device & Dtype Management
```python
# Move tensors to correct device/dtype for add_noise:
self.alphas_cumprod = self.alphas_cumprod.to(device=original_samples.device)
alphas_cumprod = self.alphas_cumprod.to(dtype=original_samples.dtype)
timesteps = timesteps.to(original_samples.device)
```

#### Pattern 5: Variance Computation Helper
```python
def _get_variance(self, t: int, ...) -> torch.Tensor:
    # Helper methods prefixed with _ are internal
    # Used by step() to compute noise variance
```

#### Pattern 6: Thresholding & Clipping
```python
if self.config.thresholding:
    pred_original_sample = self._threshold_sample(pred_original_sample)
elif self.config.clip_sample:
    pred_original_sample = pred_original_sample.clamp(-self.config.clip_sample_range, self.config.clip_sample_range)
```

#### Pattern 7: Custom Timesteps Support
```python
if self.custom_timesteps:
    # Use index lookup for custom timesteps
    index = (self.timesteps == timestep).nonzero(as_tuple=True)[0][0]
else:
    # Use default decrement
    prev_t = timestep - 1
```

### 8. DPMSolverMultistepScheduler (Advanced Example)

**Extended Init Parameters (Lines 214-245):**

- `algorithm_type`: "dpmsolver", "dpmsolver++", "sde-dpmsolver", "sde-dpmsolver++"
- `solver_type`: "midpoint" or "heun"
- `use_karras_sigmas`, `use_exponential_sigmas`, `use_beta_sigmas`, `use_lu_lambdas`, `use_flow_sigmas`
- `flow_shift`, `lambda_min_clipped`, `variance_type`
- Validation checks happen in __init__ (lines 246-267)

**Pattern:** More complex schedulers extend the basic config with solver-specific options while maintaining the same inheritance and decorator patterns.

---

### 9. FILE PATHS SUMMARY

**Critical Files:**
- `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py` - SchedulerMixin base class (190 lines)
- `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddpm.py` - DDPM implementation (669 lines)
- `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddim.py` - DDIM implementation (596 lines)
- `/src/diffusers/configuration_utils.py` - ConfigMixin and @register_to_config (798 lines)
- `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_dpmsolver_multistep.py` - Advanced scheduler example (600+ lines)
- `/src/diffusers/utils/outputs.py` - BaseOutput dataclass (line 40+)

---

### 10. INITIALIZATION WORKFLOW SUMMARY

1. **Create scheduler instance:**
   ```python
   scheduler = DDPMScheduler(num_train_timesteps=1000, ...)
   ```

2. **@register_to_config decorator intercepts:**
   - Captures all parameters from `__init__` signature
   - Calls `self.register_to_config(**kwargs)`
   - Stores in `self._internal_dict` (FrozenDict)
   - Then calls original `__init__` implementation

3. **Original __init__ executes:**
   - Computes beta schedules
   - Calculates alphas, alphas_cumprod
   - Initializes instance variables (timesteps, init_noise_sigma, etc.)
   - Sets up variance_type behavior

4. **Before inference:**
   - Call `scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)`
   - Sets up `self.timesteps` tensor for denoising loop

5. **Inference loop:**
   - Get model prediction: `model_output = model(sample, timestep)`
   - Call `scheduler.step()` to get `prev_sample`
   - Update sample and continue

6. **Config persistence:**
   - `scheduler.save_pretrained(directory)` saves to `scheduler_config.json`
   - `DDPMScheduler.from_pretrained(directory)` loads from JSON

This architecture provides a clean, extensible interface while automatically managing configuration serialization and compatibility across different scheduler variants.

Perfect! Now let me compile a comprehensive report with all the findings.

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Scheduler Implementation in Hugging Face Diffusers

I've conducted a thorough exploration of the scheduler architecture in the Hugging Face diffusers library. Here are my detailed findings:

### 1. SCHEDULER FILES DIRECTORY

The `/src/diffusers/schedulers/` directory contains **57 scheduler files**:

**Core Base Classes:**
- `scheduling_utils.py` - Base classes and utilities
- `configuration_utils.py` (parent) - Configuration management

**Main Schedulers (PyTorch):**
- `scheduling_ddpm.py` - Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
- `scheduling_ddim.py` - DDIM variant
- `scheduling_dpmsolver_multistep.py` - DPM-Solver multi-step
- `scheduling_euler_discrete.py` - Euler method
- `scheduling_heun_discrete.py` - Heun method
- `scheduling_pndm.py` - PNDM scheduler
- And 40+ additional specialized schedulers

**Flax Variants (JAX):**
- `scheduling_ddpm_flax.py`
- `scheduling_ddim_flax.py`
- `scheduling_euler_discrete_flax.py`
- And others for different backends

### 2. ARCHITECTURE & INHERITANCE

**Class Hierarchy Pattern:**
```python
class [SchedulerName]Scheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
    ...
```

**Base Classes Location:**
- `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py` - SchedulerMixin
- `/src/diffusers/configuration_utils.py` - ConfigMixin

### 3. DETAILED SCHEDULER IMPLEMENTATION (DDPMScheduler Example)

**File:** `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddpm.py`

#### Class Structure:
```python
class DDPMScheduler(SchedulerMixin, ConfigMixin):
    _compatibles = [e.name for e in KarrasDiffusionSchedulers]
    order = 1
```

#### `@register_to_config` Decorator:
Applied to `__init__` method. This decorator automatically registers all init parameters to the config dictionary, which allows:
- Config saving/loading
- Parameter serialization to JSON
- Automatic state persistence

#### `__init__` Signature (Lines 186-211):
```python
@register_to_config
def __init__(
    self,
    num_train_timesteps: int = 1000,
    beta_start: float = 0.0001,
    beta_end: float = 0.02,
    beta_schedule: Literal["linear", "scaled_linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2", "sigmoid"] = "linear",
    trained_betas: np.ndarray | list[float] | None = None,
    variance_type: Literal[...] = "fixed_small",
    clip_sample: bool = True,
    prediction_type: Literal["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"] = "epsilon",
    thresholding: bool = False,
    dynamic_thresholding_ratio: float = 0.995,
    clip_sample_range: float = 1.0,
    sample_max_value: float = 1.0,
    timestep_spacing: Literal["linspace", "leading", "trailing"] = "leading",
    steps_offset: int = 0,
    rescale_betas_zero_snr: bool = False,
):
```

#### Beta Schedule Computation (Lines 212-241):
The scheduler supports multiple beta schedule types:
- **"linear"**: `torch.linspace(beta_start, beta_end, num_train_timesteps)`
- **"scaled_linear"**: Square root scaling for latent diffusion
- **"squaredcos_cap_v2"**: Glide cosine schedule via `betas_for_alpha_bar()`
- **"sigmoid"**: GeoDiff sigmoid schedule
- **"trained_betas"**: Custom pre-computed betas array

**Helper Function:** `betas_for_alpha_bar()` (Lines 48-97):
- Creates beta schedule from alpha_bar function
- Supports "cosine", "laplace", "exp" alpha transform types
- Returns torch.Tensor of shape (num_diffusion_timesteps,)

**Zero SNR Rescaling:** `rescale_zero_terminal_snr()` (Lines 101-134):
- Rescales betas for improved sample quality
- Enables very bright and dark samples

#### Alpha Computations (Lines 243-245):
```python
self.alphas = 1.0 - self.betas
self.alphas_cumprod = torch.cumprod(self.alphas, dim=0)  # Cumulative product
```

#### Key Instance Variables Set in `__init__`:
```python
self.init_noise_sigma = 1.0  # Standard deviation of initial noise
self.custom_timesteps = False
self.num_inference_steps = None
self.timesteps = torch.from_numpy(np.arange(0, num_train_timesteps)[::-1].copy())
self.variance_type = variance_type
```

#### `set_timesteps()` Method (Lines 274-346):

**Signature:**
```python
def set_timesteps(
    self,
    num_inference_steps: int = None,
    device: str | torch.device = None,
    timesteps: list[int] | None = None,
) -> None:
```

**Functionality:**
- Sets discrete timesteps for the denoising chain
- **Must be called before inference**
- Supports custom timesteps or automatic spacing
- Three spacing strategies matching Table 2 of https://huggingface.co/papers/2305.08891:
  - **"linspace"**: Evenly spaced across range
  - **"leading"**: Leading edge spacing (step_ratio = num_train_timesteps // num_inference_steps)
  - **"trailing"**: Trailing edge spacing

#### `step()` Method (Lines 461-567):

**Signature:**
```python
def step(
    self,
    model_output: torch.Tensor,
    timestep: int,
    sample: torch.Tensor,
    generator: torch.Generator | None = None,
    return_dict: bool = True,
) -> DDPMSchedulerOutput | tuple:
```

**Process (7 steps):**
1. Extract variance predictions from model output (if using learned variance)
2. Compute alpha products at current and previous timesteps
3. Convert model output to predicted original sample based on `prediction_type`:
   - "epsilon": `(sample - sqrt(beta_prod_t) * model_output) / sqrt(alpha_prod_t)`
   - "sample": Use directly
   - "v_prediction": `(sqrt(alpha_prod_t) * sample) - (sqrt(beta_prod_t) * model_output)`
4. Apply thresholding or clipping to predicted sample
5. Compute coefficients for prev_sample computation
6. Compute predicted previous sample µ_t
7. Add noise (Gaussian noise scaled by variance)

**Return Type:** `DDPMSchedulerOutput` dataclass (Lines 31-45):
```python
@dataclass
class DDPMSchedulerOutput(BaseOutput):
    prev_sample: torch.Tensor
    pred_original_sample: torch.Tensor | None = None
```

#### `add_noise()` Method (Lines 569-609):

**Signature:**
```python
def add_noise(
    self,
    original_samples: torch.Tensor,
    noise: torch.Tensor,
    timesteps: torch.IntTensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
```

**Process (Forward Diffusion):**
```python
sqrt_alpha_prod = alphas_cumprod[timesteps] ** 0.5
sqrt_one_minus_alpha_prod = (1 - alphas_cumprod[timesteps]) ** 0.5
noisy_samples = sqrt_alpha_prod * original_samples + sqrt_one_minus_alpha_prod * noise
```

**Pattern:** Reshape to broadcast correctly across batch dimensions.

#### Helper Methods:

**`_get_variance()` (Lines 348-416):**
- Computes variance for given timestep
- Supports 6 variance types: fixed_small, fixed_small_log, fixed_large, fixed_large_log, learned, learned_range

**`_threshold_sample()` (Lines 418-459):**
- Dynamic thresholding based on quantile values
- Prevents pixel saturation

**`previous_timestep()` (Lines 648-668):**
- Computes previous timestep accounting for custom timesteps

**`scale_model_input()` (Lines 257-272):**
- Hook for schedulers that need input scaling (DDPM returns sample as-is)

**`get_velocity()` (Lines 611-643):**
- Computes velocity from sample and noise for v_prediction training

#### Other Methods:
- `__len__()` - Returns `config.num_train_timesteps`

---

- `flow_shift`, `lambda_min_clipped`, `variance_type`
- Validation checks happen in __init__ (lines 246-267)

**Pattern:** More complex schedulers extend the basic config with solver-specific options while maintaining the same inheritance and decorator patterns.

---

### 9. FILE PATHS SUMMARY

**Critical Files:**
- `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_utils.py` - SchedulerMixin base class (190 lines)
- `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddpm.py` - DDPM implementation (669 lines)
- `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_ddim.py` - DDIM implementation (596 lines)
- `/src/diffusers/configuration_utils.py` - ConfigMixin and @register_to_config (798 lines)
- `/src/diffusers/schedulers/scheduling_dpmsolver_multistep.py` - Advanced scheduler example (600+ lines)
- `/src/diffusers/utils/outputs.py` - BaseOutput dataclass (line 40+)

---

### 10. INITIALIZATION WORKFLOW SUMMARY

1. **Create scheduler instance:**
   ```python
   scheduler = DDPMScheduler(num_train_timesteps=1000, ...)
   ```

2. **@register_to_config decorator intercepts:**
   - Captures all parameters from `__init__` signature
   - Calls `self.register_to_config(**kwargs)`
   - Stores in `self._internal_dict` (FrozenDict)
   - Then calls original `__init__` implementation

3. **Original __init__ executes:**
   - Computes beta schedules
   - Calculates alphas, alphas_cumprod
   - Initializes instance variables (timesteps, init_noise_sigma, etc.)
   - Sets up variance_type behavior

4. **Before inference:**
   - Call `scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)`
   - Sets up `self.timesteps` tensor for denoising loop

5. **Inference loop:**
   - Get model prediction: `model_output = model(sample, timestep)`
   - Call `scheduler.step()` to get `prev_sample`
   - Update sample and continue

6. **Config persistence:**
   - `scheduler.save_pretrained(directory)` saves to `scheduler_config.json`
   - `DDPMScheduler.from_pretrained(directory)` loads from JSON

This architecture provides a clean, extensible interface while automatically managing configuration serialization and compatibility across different scheduler variants.

65	    Args:

74	class SchedulerMixin(PushToHubMixin):
75	    """
76	    Base class for all schedulers.
77	
78	    [`SchedulerMixin`] contains common functions shared by all schedulers such as general loading and saving
79	    functionalities.
80	
81	    [`ConfigMixin`] takes care of storing the configuration attributes (like `num_train_timesteps`) that are passed to
82	    the scheduler's `__init__` function, and the attributes can be accessed by `scheduler.config.num_train_timesteps`.
83	
84	    Class attributes:
85	        - **_compatibles** (`list[str]`) -- A list of scheduler classes that are compatible with the parent scheduler
86	          class. Use [`~ConfigMixin.from_config`] to load a different compatible scheduler class (should be overridden
87	          by parent class).
88	    """
89	
90	    config_name = SCHEDULER_CONFIG_NAME
91	    _compatibles = []
92	    has_compatibles = True
93	
94	    @classmethod
95	    @validate_hf_hub_args
96	    def from_pretrained(
97	        cls,
98	        pretrained_model_name_or_path: str | os.PathLike | None = None,
99	        subfolder: str | None = None,
100	        return_unused_kwargs=False,
101	        **kwargs,
102	    ) -> Self:
103	        r"""
104	        Instantiate a scheduler from a pre-defined JSON configuration file in a local directory or Hub repository.
105	
106	        Parameters:
107	            pretrained_model_name_or_path (`str` or `os.PathLike`, *optional*):
108	                Can be either:
109	
110	                    - A string, the *model id* (for example `google/ddpm-celebahq-256`) of a pretrained model hosted on
111	                      the Hub.
112	                    - A path to a *directory* (for example `./my_model_directory`) containing the scheduler
113	                      configuration saved with [`~SchedulerMixin.save_pretrained`].
114	            subfolder (`str`, *optional*):
115	                The subfolder location of a model file within a larger model repository on the Hub or locally.
116	            return_unused_kwargs (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
117	                Whether kwargs that are not consumed by the Python class should be returned or not.
118	            cache_dir (`str | os.PathLike`, *optional*):
119	                Path to a directory where a downloaded pretrained model configuration is cached if the standard cache
120	                is not used.
121	            force_download (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
122	                Whether or not to force the (re-)download of the model weights and configuration files, overriding the
123	                cached versions if they exist.
124	
125	            proxies (`dict[str, str]`, *optional*):
126	                A dictionary of proxy servers to use by protocol or endpoint, for example, `{'http': 'foo.bar:3128',
127	                'http://hostname': 'foo.bar:4012'}`. The proxies are used on each request.
128	            output_loading_info(`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `False`):
129	                Whether or not to also return a dictionary containing missing keys, unexpected keys and error messages.

8	class DDPMSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):

11	    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):
12	        config = {
13	            "num_train_timesteps": 1000,
14	            "beta_start": 0.0001,
15	            "beta_end": 0.02,
16	            "beta_schedule": "linear",
17	            "variance_type": "fixed_small",
18	            "clip_sample": True,
19	        }
20	
21	        config.update(**kwargs)
22	        return config
23	
24	    def test_timesteps(self):
25	        for timesteps in [1, 5, 100, 1000]:
26	            self.check_over_configs(num_train_timesteps=timesteps)
27	
28	    def test_betas(self):
29	        for beta_start, beta_end in zip([0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1], [0.002, 0.02, 0.2, 2]):
30	            self.check_over_configs(beta_start=beta_start, beta_end=beta_end)
31	
32	    def test_schedules(self):
33	        for schedule in ["linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2"]:
34	            self.check_over_configs(beta_schedule=schedule)
35	
36	    def test_variance_type(self):
37	        for variance in ["fixed_small", "fixed_large", "other"]:
38	            self.check_over_configs(variance_type=variance)
39	
40	    def test_clip_sample(self):
41	        for clip_sample in [True, False]:
42	            self.check_over_configs(clip_sample=clip_sample)
43	
44	    def test_thresholding(self):
45	        self.check_over_configs(thresholding=False)
46	        for threshold in [0.5, 1.0, 2.0]:
47	            for prediction_type in ["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"]:
48	                self.check_over_configs(
49	                    thresholding=True,
50	                    prediction_type=prediction_type,
51	                    sample_max_value=threshold,
52	                )
53	
54	    def test_prediction_type(self):
55	        for prediction_type in ["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"]:
56	            self.check_over_configs(prediction_type=prediction_type)
57	
58	    def test_time_indices(self):

60	            self.check_over_forward(time_step=t)

62	    def test_variance(self):

64	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()

66

69	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(999) - 0.02)) < 1e-5

79

81

84	        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)

90	            # 2. predict previous mean of sample x_t-1

92	
93	            # if t > 0:

358	    def dummy_model(self):
359	        def model(sample, t, *args):
360	            # if t is a tensor, match the number of dimensions of sample
361	            if isinstance(t, torch.Tensor):
362	                num_dims = len(sample.shape)
363	                # pad t with 1s to match num_dims
364	                t = t.reshape(-1, *(1,) * (num_dims - 1)).to(sample.device, dtype=sample.dtype)
365	
366	            return sample * t / (t + 1)
367	
368	        return model

370	    def check_over_configs(self, time_step=0, **config):
371	        kwargs = dict(self.forward_default_kwargs)
372	
373	        num_inference_steps = kwargs.pop("num_inference_steps", None)
374	        time_step = time_step if time_step is not None else self.default_timestep
375	
376	        for scheduler_class in self.scheduler_classes:
377	            # TODO(Suraj) - delete the following two lines once DDPM, DDIM, and PNDM have timesteps casted to float by default
378	            if scheduler_class in (EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler, EulerDiscreteScheduler, LMSDiscreteScheduler):
379	                time_step = float(time_step)
380	
381	            scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
382	            scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
383	
384	            if scheduler_class == CMStochasticIterativeScheduler:
385	                # Get valid timestep based on sigma_max, which should always be in timestep schedule.
386	                scaled_sigma_max = scheduler.sigma_to_t(scheduler.config.sigma_max)
387	                time_step = scaled_sigma_max
388	
389	            if scheduler_class == EDMEulerScheduler:
390	                time_step = scheduler.timesteps[-1]
391	
392	            if scheduler_class == VQDiffusionScheduler:
393	                num_vec_classes = scheduler_config["num_vec_classes"]
394	                sample = self.dummy_sample(num_vec_classes)
395	                model = self.dummy_model(num_vec_classes)
396	                residual = model(sample, time_step)
397	            else:
398	                sample = self.dummy_sample
399	                residual = 0.1 * sample
400	
401	            with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdirname:
402	                scheduler.save_config(tmpdirname)
403	                new_scheduler = scheduler_class.from_pretrained(tmpdirname)
404	
405	            if num_inference_steps is not None and hasattr(scheduler, "set_timesteps"):
406	                scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
407	                new_scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
408	            elif num_inference_steps is not None and not hasattr(scheduler, "set_timesteps"):
409	                kwargs["num_inference_steps"] = num_inference_steps
410	
411	            # Make sure `scale_model_input` is invoked to prevent a warning
412	            if scheduler_class == CMStochasticIterativeScheduler:
413	                # Get valid timestep based on sigma_max, which should always be in timestep schedule.
414	                _ = scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, scaled_sigma_max)
415	                _ = new_scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, scaled_sigma_max)
416	            elif scheduler_class != VQDiffusionScheduler:
417	                _ = scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, scheduler.timesteps[-1])
418	                _ = new_scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, scheduler.timesteps[-1])
419	
420	            # Set the seed before step() as some schedulers are stochastic like EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler, EulerDiscreteScheduler
421	            if "generator" in set(inspect.signature(scheduler.step).parameters.keys()):
422	                kwargs["generator"] = torch.manual_seed(0)
423	            output = scheduler.step(residual, time_step, sample, **kwargs).prev_sample
424	
425	            if "generator" in set(inspect.signature(scheduler.step).parameters.keys()):
426	                kwargs["generator"] = torch.manual_seed(0)
427	            new_output = new_scheduler.step(residual, time_step, sample, **kwargs).prev_sample
428	
429	            assert torch.sum(torch.abs(output - new_output)) < 1e-5, "Scheduler outputs are not identical"

596	    def test_scheduler_outputs_equivalence(self):
597	        def set_nan_tensor_to_zero(t):
598	            t[t != t] = 0
599	            return t
600	
601	        def recursive_check(tuple_object, dict_object):
602	            if isinstance(tuple_object, (List, Tuple)):
603	                for tuple_iterable_value, dict_iterable_value in zip(tuple_object, dict_object.values()):
604	                    recursive_check(tuple_iterable_value, dict_iterable_value)
605	            elif isinstance(tuple_object, Dict):
606	                for tuple_iterable_value, dict_iterable_value in zip(tuple_object.values(), dict_object.values()):
607	                    recursive_check(tuple_iterable_value, dict_iterable_value)
608	            elif tuple_object is None:
609	                return
610	            else:
611	                self.assertTrue(
612	                    torch.allclose(
613	                        set_nan_tensor_to_zero(tuple_object), set_nan_tensor_to_zero(dict_object), atol=1e-5
614	                    ),
615	                    msg=(
616	                        "Tuple and dict output are not equal. Difference:"
617	                        f" {torch.max(torch.abs(tuple_object - dict_object))}. Tuple has `nan`:"
618	                        f" {torch.isnan(tuple_object).any()} and `inf`: {torch.isinf(tuple_object)}. Dict has"
619	                        f" `nan`: {torch.isnan(dict_object).any()} and `inf`: {torch.isinf(dict_object)}."
620	                    ),
621	                )
622	
623	        kwargs = dict(self.forward_default_kwargs)

626	        timestep = self.default_timestep
627	        if len(self.scheduler_classes) > 0 and self.scheduler_classes[0] == IPNDMScheduler:
628	            timestep = 1
629	
630	        for scheduler_class in self.scheduler_classes:
631	            if scheduler_class in (EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler, EulerDiscreteScheduler, LMSDiscreteScheduler):
632	                timestep = float(timestep)
633	
634	            scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
635	            scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
636	
637	            if scheduler_class == CMStochasticIterativeScheduler:
638	                # Get valid timestep based on sigma_max, which should always be in timestep schedule.
639	                timestep = scheduler.sigma_to_t(scheduler.config.sigma_max)
640	
641	            if scheduler_class == VQDiffusionScheduler:
642	                num_vec_classes = scheduler_config["num_vec_classes"]
643	                sample = self.dummy_sample(num_vec_classes)
644	                model = self.dummy_model(num_vec_classes)
645	                residual = model(sample, timestep)
646	            else:
647	                sample = self.dummy_sample
648	                residual = 0.1 * sample
649	
650	            if num_inference_steps is not None and hasattr(scheduler, "set_timesteps"):
651	                scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
652	            elif num_inference_steps is not None and not hasattr(scheduler, "set_timesteps"):
653	                kwargs["num_inference_steps"] = num_inference_steps
654	
655	            # Set the seed before state as some schedulers are stochastic like EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler, EulerDiscreteScheduler
656	            if "generator" in set(inspect.signature(scheduler.step).parameters.keys()):
657	                kwargs["generator"] = torch.manual_seed(0)
658	            outputs_dict = scheduler.step(residual, timestep, sample, **kwargs)
659	
660	            if num_inference_steps is not None and hasattr(scheduler, "set_timesteps"):
661	                scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
662	            elif num_inference_steps is not None and not hasattr(scheduler, "set_timesteps"):
663	                kwargs["num_inference_steps"] = num_inference_steps
664	
665	            # Set the seed before state as some schedulers are stochastic like EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler, EulerDiscreteScheduler
666	            if "generator" in set(inspect.signature(scheduler.step).parameters.keys()):
667	                kwargs["generator"] = torch.manual_seed(0)
668	            outputs_tuple = scheduler.step(residual, timestep, sample, return_dict=False, **kwargs)

736	                raise ValueError(
737	                    f"{scheduler_class} has `**kwargs` in its __init__ method but has not defined any deprecated"
738	                    " kwargs under the `_deprecated_kwargs` class attribute. Make sure to either remove `**kwargs` if"
739	                    " there are no deprecated arguments or add the deprecated argument with `_deprecated_kwargs ="
740	                    " [<deprecated_argument>]`"
741	                )
742	
743	            if not has_kwarg_in_model_class and has_deprecated_kwarg:
744	                raise ValueError(
745	                    f"{scheduler_class} doesn't have `**kwargs` in its __init__ method but has defined deprecated"
746	                    " kwargs under the `_deprecated_kwargs` class attribute. Make sure to either add the `**kwargs`"
747	                    f" argument to {self.model_class}.__init__ if there are deprecated arguments or remove the"
748	                    " deprecated argument from `_deprecated_kwargs = [<deprecated_argument>]`"

797	            # warning should be thrown

799	                assert scheduler.test_attribute == 5

9	class EulerDiscreteSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
10	    scheduler_classes = (EulerDiscreteScheduler,)
11	    num_inference_steps = 10
12	
13	    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):
14	        config = {
15	            "num_train_timesteps": 1100,
16	            "beta_start": 0.0001,
17	            "beta_end": 0.02,
18	            "beta_schedule": "linear",
19	        }
20	
21	        config.update(**kwargs)
22	        return config
23	
24	    def test_timesteps(self):
25	        for timesteps in [10, 50, 100, 1000]:
26	            self.check_over_configs(num_train_timesteps=timesteps)
27	
28	    def test_betas(self):
29	        for beta_start, beta_end in zip([0.00001, 0.0001, 0.001], [0.0002, 0.002, 0.02]):
30	            self.check_over_configs(beta_start=beta_start, beta_end=beta_end)
31	
32	    def test_schedules(self):
33	        for schedule in ["linear", "scaled_linear"]:
34	            self.check_over_configs(beta_schedule=schedule)
35	
36	    def test_prediction_type(self):
37	        for prediction_type in ["epsilon", "v_prediction"]:
38	            self.check_over_configs(prediction_type=prediction_type)
39	
40	    def test_timestep_type(self):
41	        timestep_types = ["discrete", "continuous"]
42	        for timestep_type in timestep_types:
43	            self.check_over_configs(timestep_type=timestep_type)
44	
45	    def test_karras_sigmas(self):
46	        self.check_over_configs(use_karras_sigmas=True, sigma_min=0.02, sigma_max=700.0)
47	
48	    def test_rescale_betas_zero_snr(self):
49	        for rescale_betas_zero_snr in [True, False]:
50	            self.check_over_configs(rescale_betas_zero_snr=rescale_betas_zero_snr)
51	
52	    def full_loop(self, **config):
53	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
54	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
55	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
56	
57	        num_inference_steps = self.num_inference_steps
58	        scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
59	
60	        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
61	
62	        model = self.dummy_model()
63	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter * scheduler.init_noise_sigma
64	        sample = sample.to(torch_device)
65	
66	        for i, t in enumerate(scheduler.timesteps):
67	            sample = scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, t)
68	
69	            model_output = model(sample, t)
70	
71	            output = scheduler.step(model_output, t, sample, generator=generator)
72	            sample = output.prev_sample
73	        return sample
74	
75	    def full_loop_custom_timesteps(self, **config):
76	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
77	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
78	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
79	
80	        num_inference_steps = self.num_inference_steps
81	        scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
82	        timesteps = scheduler.timesteps
83	        # reset the timesteps using `timesteps`
84	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
85	        scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps=None, timesteps=timesteps)
86	
87	        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
88	
89	        model = self.dummy_model()
90	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter * scheduler.init_noise_sigma
91	        sample = sample.to(torch_device)
92	
93	        for i, t in enumerate(scheduler.timesteps):
94	            sample = scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, t)
95	
96	            model_output = model(sample, t)
97	
98	            output = scheduler.step(model_output, t, sample, generator=generator)
99	            sample = output.prev_sample
100	        return sample
101

129	    def test_full_loop_no_noise(self):
130	        sample = self.full_loop()
131	
132	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
133	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
134	
135	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 10.0807) < 1e-2
136	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.0131) < 1e-3
137	
138	    def test_full_loop_with_v_prediction(self):

143	
144	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 0.0002) < 1e-2
145	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 2.2676e-06) < 1e-3

201	    def test_full_loop_with_noise(self):
202	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
203	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
204	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
205	
206	        scheduler.set_timesteps(self.num_inference_steps)
207	
208	        generator = torch.manual_seed(0)
209	
210	        model = self.dummy_model()
211	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter * scheduler.init_noise_sigma
212	
213	        # add noise
214	        t_start = self.num_inference_steps - 2
215	        noise = self.dummy_noise_deter
216	        noise = noise.to(sample.device)
217	        timesteps = scheduler.timesteps[t_start * scheduler.order :]
218	        sample = scheduler.add_noise(sample, noise, timesteps[:1])
219	
220	        for i, t in enumerate(timesteps):
221	            sample = scheduler.scale_model_input(sample, t)
222	
223	            model_output = model(sample, t)
224	
225	            output = scheduler.step(model_output, t, sample, generator=generator)
226	            sample = output.prev_sample
227	
228	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
229	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
230	
231	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 57062.9297) < 1e-2, f" expected result sum 57062.9297, but get {result_sum}"
232	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 74.3007) < 1e-3, f" expected result mean 74.3007, but get {result_mean}"
233	
234	    def test_custom_timesteps(self):
235	        for prediction_type in ["epsilon", "sample", "v_prediction"]:
236	            for interpolation_type in ["linear", "log_linear"]:
237	                for final_sigmas_type in ["sigma_min", "zero"]:
238	                    sample = self.full_loop(
239	                        prediction_type=prediction_type,
240	                        interpolation_type=interpolation_type,
241	                        final_sigmas_type=final_sigmas_type,
242	                    )
243	                    sample_custom_timesteps = self.full_loop_custom_timesteps(
244	                        prediction_type=prediction_type,
245	                        interpolation_type=interpolation_type,
246	                        final_sigmas_type=final_sigmas_type,
247	                    )
248	                    assert torch.sum(torch.abs(sample - sample_custom_timesteps)) < 1e-5, (
249	                        f"Scheduler outputs are not identical for prediction_type: {prediction_type}, interpolation_type: {interpolation_type} and final_sigmas_type: {final_sigmas_type}"
250	                    )
251

8	class DDIMSchedulerTest(SchedulerCommonTest):
9	    scheduler_classes = (DDIMScheduler,)
10	    forward_default_kwargs = (("eta", 0.0), ("num_inference_steps", 50))
11	
12	    def get_scheduler_config(self, **kwargs):
13	        config = {
14	            "num_train_timesteps": 1000,
15	            "beta_start": 0.0001,
16	            "beta_end": 0.02,
17	            "beta_schedule": "linear",
18	            "clip_sample": True,
19	        }
20	
21	        config.update(**kwargs)
22	        return config
23	
24	    def full_loop(self, **config):
25	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
26	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(**config)
27	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
28	
29	        num_inference_steps, eta = 10, 0.0
30	
31	        model = self.dummy_model()
32	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
33	
34	        scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
35	
36	        for t in scheduler.timesteps:
37	            residual = model(sample, t)
38	            sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, eta).prev_sample
39	
40	        return sample
41	
42	    def test_timesteps(self):
43	        for timesteps in [100, 500, 1000]:
44	            self.check_over_configs(num_train_timesteps=timesteps)
45	
46	    def test_steps_offset(self):
47	        for steps_offset in [0, 1]:
48	            self.check_over_configs(steps_offset=steps_offset)
49	
50	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
51	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config(steps_offset=1)
52	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
53	        scheduler.set_timesteps(5)
54	        assert torch.equal(scheduler.timesteps, torch.LongTensor([801, 601, 401, 201, 1]))
55	
56	    def test_betas(self):
57	        for beta_start, beta_end in zip([0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.1], [0.002, 0.02, 0.2, 2]):
58	            self.check_over_configs(beta_start=beta_start, beta_end=beta_end)
59	
60	    def test_schedules(self):
61	        for schedule in ["linear", "squaredcos_cap_v2"]:
62	            self.check_over_configs(beta_schedule=schedule)
63	
64	    def test_prediction_type(self):
65	        for prediction_type in ["epsilon", "v_prediction"]:
66	            self.check_over_configs(prediction_type=prediction_type)
67	
68	    def test_clip_sample(self):
69	        for clip_sample in [True, False]:
70	            self.check_over_configs(clip_sample=clip_sample)
71	
72	    def test_timestep_spacing(self):
73	        for timestep_spacing in ["trailing", "leading"]:
74	            self.check_over_configs(timestep_spacing=timestep_spacing)
75	
76	    def test_rescale_betas_zero_snr(self):
77	        for rescale_betas_zero_snr in [True, False]:
78	            self.check_over_configs(rescale_betas_zero_snr=rescale_betas_zero_snr)
79	
80	    def test_thresholding(self):
81	        self.check_over_configs(thresholding=False)
82	        for threshold in [0.5, 1.0, 2.0]:
83	            for prediction_type in ["epsilon", "v_prediction"]:
84	                self.check_over_configs(
85	                    thresholding=True,
86	                    prediction_type=prediction_type,
87	                    sample_max_value=threshold,
88	                )
89	
90	    def test_time_indices(self):
91	        for t in [1, 10, 49]:
92	            self.check_over_forward(time_step=t)
93	
94	    def test_inference_steps(self):
95	        for t, num_inference_steps in zip([1, 10, 50], [10, 50, 500]):
96	            self.check_over_forward(time_step=t, num_inference_steps=num_inference_steps)
97	
98	    def test_eta(self):
99	        for t, eta in zip([1, 10, 49], [0.0, 0.5, 1.0]):
100	            self.check_over_forward(time_step=t, eta=eta)
101	
102	    def test_variance(self):
103	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
104	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
105	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
106	
107	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(0, 0) - 0.0)) < 1e-5
108	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(420, 400) - 0.14771)) < 1e-5
109	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(980, 960) - 0.32460)) < 1e-5
110	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(0, 0) - 0.0)) < 1e-5
111	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(487, 486) - 0.00979)) < 1e-5
112	        assert torch.sum(torch.abs(scheduler._get_variance(999, 998) - 0.02)) < 1e-5
113	
114	    def test_full_loop_no_noise(self):
115	        sample = self.full_loop()
116	
117	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
118	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
119	
120	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 172.0067) < 1e-2
121	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.223967) < 1e-3
122	
123	    def test_full_loop_with_v_prediction(self):
124	        sample = self.full_loop(prediction_type="v_prediction")
125	
126	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
127	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
128	
129	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 52.5302) < 1e-2
130	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.0684) < 1e-3
131	
132	    def test_full_loop_with_set_alpha_to_one(self):
133	        # We specify different beta, so that the first alpha is 0.99
134	        sample = self.full_loop(set_alpha_to_one=True, beta_start=0.01)
135	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
136	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
137	
138	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 149.8295) < 1e-2
139	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.1951) < 1e-3
140	
141	    def test_full_loop_with_no_set_alpha_to_one(self):
142	        # We specify different beta, so that the first alpha is 0.99
143	        sample = self.full_loop(set_alpha_to_one=False, beta_start=0.01)
144	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
145	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
146	
147	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 149.0784) < 1e-2
148	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.1941) < 1e-3
149	
150	    def test_full_loop_with_noise(self):
151	        scheduler_class = self.scheduler_classes[0]
152	        scheduler_config = self.get_scheduler_config()
153	        scheduler = scheduler_class(**scheduler_config)
154	
155	        num_inference_steps, eta = 10, 0.0
156	        t_start = 8
157	
158	        model = self.dummy_model()
159	        sample = self.dummy_sample_deter
160	
161	        scheduler.set_timesteps(num_inference_steps)
162	
163	        # add noise
164	        noise = self.dummy_noise_deter
165	        timesteps = scheduler.timesteps[t_start * scheduler.order :]
166	        sample = scheduler.add_noise(sample, noise, timesteps[:1])
167	
168	        for t in timesteps:
169	            residual = model(sample, t)
170	            sample = scheduler.step(residual, t, sample, eta).prev_sample
171	
172	        result_sum = torch.sum(torch.abs(sample))
173	        result_mean = torch.mean(torch.abs(sample))
174	
175	        assert abs(result_sum.item() - 354.5418) < 1e-2, f" expected result sum 218.4379, but get {result_sum}"
176	        assert abs(result_mean.item() - 0.4616) < 1e-3, f" expected result mean 0.2844, but get {result_mean}"

18	    forward_default_kwargs = (("num_inference_steps", 25),)
